message:
feat(fnp-python): re-export 50 numpy dtype scalar types (br-franken_numpy-k74v.4)

Expose numpy dtype scalars (bool_, int8..int64, uint8..uint64, float16,
float32, float64, complex64, complex128, datetime64, timedelta64, and
the abstract supers — floating, integer, signedinteger, unsignedinteger,
complexfloating, inexact, number, flexible, generic, character, void,
object_, etc.) as fnp_python attributes via numpy passthrough. 50+ names
iterated from a const slice with per-name getattr so
`fnp_python.int8 is numpy.int8` holds — users can pass e.g.
`fnp_python.int8` directly as dtype= to any wrapper.

Platform-optional types (float128, complex256, bool) are gracefully
skipped on builds where numpy itself does not expose them; the whole
block is also skipped if numpy is unimportable (matches the rest of the
fnp-python parity-oracle surface).

Test: module_reexports_numpy_dtype_scalar_types_matching_numpy asserts
identity (`ours.is(&theirs)`) for every universal_required name and
checks the platform-optional group in both directions: if numpy exposes
it we must too, if numpy does not we must also not. Verified via
rch exec -- cargo test -p fnp-python --lib module_: 4 passed, 0 failed
(including the 2 earlier constants/version parity tests).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
